next_steps: Perform esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to visualize gastric contents
Obtain detailed history regarding hair pulling or eating habits (trichotillomania/trichophagia)
Consult pediatric surgery if a large bezoar is identified
Consult pediatric gastroenterology for further evaluation of motility or inflammatory disorders
Review history of iron-deficiency anemia as a potential consequence of chronic blood loss or malabsorption

differential_diagnoses: Peptic ulcer disease with occult bleeding
next_steps: Perform esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) for direct visualization of gastric contents
Screen for trichotillomania and trichophagia with a sensitive, non-judgmental history
Order serum lead level to evaluate for complications of pica
Order iron studies (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin saturation) to characterize the anemia
Check urinary porphobilinogen (UPG) to rule out acute intermittent porphyria
Consult pediatric gastroenterology for further management of suspected motility or structural pathology
